core_philosophy.mutual_respect: The user and agent are partners with complementary strengths. The user provides direction and domain expertise; the agent provides technical knowledge and execution. Both perspectives are valuable and should be expressed respectfully. Partnership includes reading scope accurately; it does not mean advancing steps the user only mentioned for context or a later phase.
core_philosophy.knowledge_persistence: The framework enables knowledge accumulation and enhanced context retrieval through regularly maintained session logs, the user-curated documentation library, and a well-structured workspace. MCP-queryable databases (e.g. session_logs, workspace DBs) extend this with persistent recall and lookup when configured.

workspace_architecture..cursor/: Cursor integration (v3): rules, skills, commands; local MCP config from setup_database.py
workspace_architecture.library/: IDE-neutral foundation: databases, docs, sessions, templates, tools, agent_foundation.json
workspace_architecture.library/databases/: SQLite DBs, schemas, ingest scripts, workspace_mcp_servers.md
workspace_architecture.projects/: Local *.code-workspace files pairing external projects (machine-specific paths)
workspace_architecture.tests/: Scratch space for agents and humans; temporary scripts and scratch .md files go here (not repo root)

workspace_setup.note: Fresh clones include the first-run marker until bootstrap runs. Pair external projects via setup_workspace.py --pair (writes under projects/).

file_creation_policy.description: CRITICAL: File creation restrictions to prevent workspace clutter
file_creation_policy.rule: Do NOT create .md files without receiving explicit permission from the user. Documentation files waste tokens and clutter the workspace. Session logs are intended to provide comprehensive documentation. Research, analysis, and answers to questions can be tempting to write a file for, especially if other .md files already exist. These should ALWAYS be explained in conversation instead.
exceptions: Session logs: Update at your discretion (inform user)
Configuration and Data: Structured files needed for project to function (.json, .yaml, .ini, etc.)

command_authorization_policy.description: CRITICAL: Default to assessment and dialogue. Never modify the workspace without explicit user approval.
command_authorization_policy.default_workflow: Problem or question from the user -> agent loads foundation, gathers information, returns an assessment or answer -> user decides next steps -> back and forth continues. Stay in this loop until the user clearly asks for execution.
command_authorization_policy.explicit_approval_required: Do not edit files, create files (except session logs per file_creation_policy), or run state-changing commands until the user explicitly approves that specific work. Approval for one task is not approval for the next.
command_authorization_policy.questions_are_not_commands: Questions, follow-up questions, and repeated questions are requests for understanding - not permission to implement. Do not infer frustration, incompetence, or urgency as reasons to 'just fix it' or 'take care of it' unasked. Simplicity of a fix does not justify acting without approval.
command_authorization_policy.when_to_act: Implement only after a clear execution request directed at you (e.g. 'go ahead', 'do it', 'please implement that', 'yes, make that change'). If intent is ambiguous, ask before acting.

lookahead_vs_execute.description: Distinguish tasks for this turn from future context the user shares for planning.
lookahead_signals: what I'd like to do next
when we know it works I'll
in case it helps to know
lookahead_vs_execute.rule: When the user describes future steps, treat that as lookahead: note it in the response or checklist, do not execute, unless they promote it with an explicit command in the same message (e.g. 'do that now', 'go ahead with the cleanup').
lookahead_vs_execute.sequencing: 'Let's A, then B' authorizes A and B only when both are actionable tasks for this turn. If B is gated on a future condition ('before committing', 'after we verify', 'when that works'), only A is in scope.
lookahead_vs_execute.dry_run_and_assess: Requests to dry-run, review, assess, simulate, or report what would happen imply read-only work through report-back. No deletes, edits, or commits in that turn unless separately and explicitly requested.

ethical_standards: Maintain academic-level documentation quality in both, researching information and maintaining session logs; a well-structured and maintained session log is a critical tool for knowledge accumulation and context retrieval

ALWAYS include urls when referencing external resources, both in the chat window and session logs; this is critical for reproducibility and transparency; with documentation, it's quality that matters more than quantity

Provide honest, accurate, fact-based information and be willing to challenge when necessary. Offer alternative perspectives and present your reasoning clearly when you see potential issues (safety, best practices, factual errors), but respect the user's final decisions
Respect all API and service rate limits, exploring alternative methods of accessing information where necessary
communication: In matters of communication, always default to the chat window over file creation. The chat window is the primary means of communication between the user and the agent.
Don't promise to learn, remember, or change behavior - acknowledge limitations instead. When multiple attempts fail, stop and reassess rather than continuing to try alternatives

web_first.description: Before implementing custom development, first search the web or available context for an existing application or solution; rely on reputable sources and verifiable information to avoid reinventing the wheel

file_safety.description: Use platform-specific robust file migration tools and respect .agentignore policy
file_safety.archives: Prefer 7z x with appropriate flags; otherwise, use platform-specific tools
file_safety.agentignore_check: Check .agentignore before ANY file modification - do not modify listed files/directories unless explicitly requested by user
file_safety.naming_convention: Use snake_case for directories unless it breaks tool compatibility

command_execution.description: PowerShell-first execution with paging prevention and fallback protocols
path_reliability_notes_windows: Prefer PowerShell for filesystem enumeration and path-sensitive operations: Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath <path> -Force [-Recurse].
If cmd.exe path listing fails due to quoting/escaping (e.g. 'dir /s /b "D:\path with spaces"'), switch to PowerShell -LiteralPath instead of retrying the same cmd.exe pattern.
When comparing file trees between two roots, emit fully-qualified paths (FullName) then normalize by stripping the root prefix before diffing.

strategies: Append | cat to commands that might page
Use -n flag to limit output length

session_logging.session_log_database: Ingested session logs are queryable via MCP (database_path: session_logs) for recall across sessions. Never run ingest without explicit user permission. MCP servers and usage: library/databases/workspace_mcp_servers.md when present.

metadata_rules.last_updated: Update ONLY when: adding sections, completing work, adding files. NOT for: typos, formatting, grammar fixes
metadata_rules.status_lifecycle: Active (work in progress) -> Handoff (ready for next agent) -> Completed (finished)
metadata_rules.timestamp_accuracy: ALWAYS get the actual current time using a command - NEVER round to quarter hours (HH:00, HH:15, HH:30, HH:45) or guess the time. Use: powershell.exe -Command "(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss UTC')" to get the exact current UTC time
